name: Zapier API / Interfaces
what_it_does: Zapier's API for triggering, managing, and integrating with Zapier's no-code workflow automation platform, enabling 7000+ app integrations through webhooks and REST hooks.
use_cases: Triggering Zapier workflows from custom applications via webhooks
Building integrations for Zapier's app marketplace (Zapier Platform)
Catching webhook events from third-party apps via Zapier
Using Zapier as a middleware layer for app-to-app automation
Monitoring and managing Zap execution history programmatically
not_for: Complex multi-step workflows with heavy data transformation (use Make/n8n)
High-frequency automation (Zapier has execution rate limits)
Teams needing code-level control over automation logic
best_when: Non-technical users need to connect apps, or you need a simple webhook-driven trigger into a large ecosystem of pre-built integrations.
avoid_when: You need complex logic, code-level control, high execution frequency, or cost-effective bulk processing.

auth.notes: Zapier Platform uses OAuth2 for app integrations. Webhooks use URL-based auth (no header signing on incoming webhooks by default). Partner API for Zap management requires OAuth2.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 100 tasks/month, 5 Zaps, single-step Zaps only
pricing.notes: Per-task pricing where each app action counts as one task. Multi-step Zaps require paid plan. Enterprise available for organizations. Costs can add up at scale.

performance.rate_limits: Free: 100 tasks/month; paid plans up to millions with enterprise

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Zap execution errors are shown in UI but less useful via API. Webhook trigger errors don't return detailed failure info. Platform SDK errors are better documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Each webhook trigger creates a new Zap run.
known_agent_gotchas: Free tier Zaps only fire every 15 minutes — not suitable for real-time automation without paid plan
Zapier processes data asynchronously — no immediate response with results from triggered Zaps
Webhook URLs are single-use secrets — treat them as credentials
Zap execution history is limited retention — don't rely on Zapier for audit trails
Task counting is per-action, not per-Zap — multi-step Zaps consume multiple tasks per run
GDPR compliance requires data residency settings — not default enabled
